description: Validate SFC, plugin, alias, and env changes through Vite dev/build behavior.
body: Use when changing imports, aliases, SFC compiler behavior, env usage, or build config. Do not trust unit tests alone for plugin resolution or CSS/SFC transforms. Inspect vite.config, ts/js config aliases, and Vue plugin settings. Verify build or dev-server smoke. Risk boundary: exposing server-only env values to client bundles. Use when Vue Frontend Engineering work touches visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
